title: rubus affinis subsp. rogersii
intro: Rubus affinis subsp. rogersii, also known as Rogers' Bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus affinis plant, which belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Rubus affinis subsp. rogersii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. Rubus affinis subsp. rogersii is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
